channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Biomarkers & Precision Medicine
channel.description: Weekly IBD literature: Biomarkers & Precision Medicine

item.title: Klebsiella Enrichment Is Associated with Disease Severity in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of applied microbiology - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a chronic inflammatory bowel disease. This study uniquely undertook a parallel, severity-stratified comparison of both fecal and mucosal microbiota and metabolites in UC pa...

item.title: The roles and heterogeneity of CD8+ T cells in inflammatory bowel disease: A narrative review of insights from single‑cell transcriptomics (Review).
item.description: International journal of molecular medicine - Review - The present review investigates the role and characteristics of CD8+ T cells in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) using single‑cell transcriptomics, revealing their pivotal functions and remarkable ...

item.title: Exploring the mechanism of Qinlian Yuyang Decoction in the treatment of ulcerative colitis based on multi-omics technology.
item.description: Chinese medicine - The global incidence of ulcerative colitis (UC) is increasing, yet effective clinical treatment options remain limited. Qinlian Yuyang Decoction (QYD), a modified formulation based on Gegen Qinlian...

item.title: Integrating exposomics and multi-omics with dysbiosis biomarkers for clinical and environmental connections implicated in neuropathology. Cause and cure cluses.
item.description: Toxicological sciences : an official journal of the Society of Toxicology - Multi-omic investigations into environmental effects on health and disease are aided by inclusion of microbial microbiomes with assessment of mirobes producing metabolites that differentially modul...

item.title: The therapeutic potential of Piezo1 channel-mediated ferroptosis and its inhibitor.
item.description: Apoptosis : an international journal on programmed cell death - Review - Piezo1 is a mechanically activated, non-selective cation channel characterized by its exquisite sensitivity to membrane tension and high permeability to calcium(Ca2+), enabling the conversion of me...

item.title: The role of mesencephalic astrocyte-derived neurotrophic factor in digestive diseases.
item.description: Annals of medicine - Review - Mesencephalic astrocyte-derived neurotrophic factor (MANF) is an evolutionarily conserved endoplasmic reticulum (ER)-resident and secreted protein highly expressed in the digestive system. Emerging...

item.title: Gene Network Enrichment Analysis and Its Application to Explore Enriched Immune Disease Pathways for Gene Network of Acute Myeloid Leukemia Cell Lines.
item.description: Journal of computational biology : a journal of computational molecular cell biology - Recent advances in gene network analysis have improved our understanding of complex disease mechanisms; however, interpreting estimated gene networks remains challenging. Existing methods for pathw...

item.title: Human umbilical cord-derived mesenchymal stem cell exosomes ameliorate hepatocyte ferroptosis and sepsis-associated liver injury by restoring iron homeostasis in hepatocytes.
item.description: Journal of nanobiotechnology - Mesenchymal stem cells-derived exosomes (MSCs-Exo) constitute critical mediators of intercellular communication between stem cells and other cell types. Among these, exosomes derived from human umb...

item.title: miR-126-3p serves as a biomarker of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and modulates the molecular mechanisms of IBD through regulation of DICER1.
item.description: Arab journal of gastroenterology : the official publication of the Pan-Arab Association of Gastroenterology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) imposes a dual burden, physiological and psychological, on patients. This study focused on miR-126-3p to explore its role in the progression of IBD and its potentia...

item.title: Genomic estimates of Identity-By-Descent relationships in large scale data sets.

item.title: Metabolic Engineering of Commensal Bacteria for Producing 5-Hydroxyindoleacetic Acid as a Therapeutic Strategy for Colitis.
item.description: ACS synthetic biology - Numerous studies have demonstrated a strong association between gut microbiota dysbiosis and the progression of colitis, potentially mediated by disruptions in microbial tryptophan metabolism. Targ...

item.title: The emerging influences of telomere and telomerase in inflammatory bowel disease and cancer progression.
item.description: Cancer treatment and research communications - Review - Telomerase is a key enzyme that extends telomere length and thus maintains genomic stability. In inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and colorectal cancer (CRC) to which it progresses, telomeres and t...

item.title: Non-invasive determination of disease activity in Crohn's disease by serum luminex profiling.
item.description: Scientific reports - Crohn’s disease (CD) is a type of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), and treatment depends on disease activity assessment requiring invasive procedures. We aimed to identify serum cytokines/chemokin...

item.title: Metabolomic Profiling of Fecal Samples Reveals Distinct Signatures Associated with Disease Phenotypes and Locations in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Crohn's disease is a heterogeneous, transmural inflammatory condition that can involve any segment of the gastrointestinal tract. Distinct locations (ileal, colonic, ileocolonic) and phenotypes (in...

item.title: Perturbations of Diet and Gut Signatures Persist During Remission in Crohn's Disease Despite Effective Immune Suppression.
item.description: Gastroenterology - We aimed to identify dietary and gut signals during Crohn's disease (CD) remission that differ from the healthy state to highlight targets that may improve disease control and clearance. We analyze...

item.title: Exploring the metabolic mysteries: Mechanistic insights into lactylation-mediated regulation of autoimmune diseases.
item.description: Autoimmunity reviews - Review - Autoimmune diseases are chronic disorders caused by the immune system's aberrant recognition and attack of self-tissues. Accumulating evidence has revealed that these diseases are closely related t...

item.title: Factors Associated With Loss of Fecal Biomarker Accuracy During Routine Assessment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: JGH open : an open access journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Fecal calprotectin (fCal) and fecal myeloperoxidase (fMPO) have been proposed as biomarkers for monitoring inflammatory bowel disease (IBD); however, their reliability is not fully established. Thi...

item.title: Serological and faecal markers of irritable bowel syndrome: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: EBioMedicine - The irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) has long been considered a functional disorder, but recent work has demonstrated clear biological signatures in immune, microbiome and enteric nervous systems of ...

item.title: Exosome genomics in inflammatory bowel disease: mechanisms and diagnostic potential.
item.description: European journal of medical research - Review - Exosomes, as crucial mediators of intercellular communication, shuttle a variety of biomolecules including lipids, proteins, and RNAs. In recent years, research has focused on their diagnostic and ...

item.title: Concomitant 5-Aminosalicylic Acid Does Not Affect the Efficacy of Janus Kinase Inhibitors in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Clinical pharmacology and therapeutics - We evaluated whether concomitant 5-aminosalicylic acid (5-ASA) influences clinical remission in patients with ulcerative colitis (UC) receiving Janus kinase inhibitors (JAKi). In this retrospective...

item.title: T cell and cytokine signatures as early predictors of response to IL-12/IL-23 inhibition in Crohn's disease.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Crohn's disease (CD) is a disabling inflammatory disorder with highly variable response to biologic therapies. Despite the availability of multiple biologic agents, a significant proportion of pati...

item.title: Nutritional Indices as Non-Invasive Biomarkers: PNI and CONUT Scores Predict Clinical and Endoscopic Activity in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Nutrients - Background and Objectives: Determination of disease activity is important for appropriate treatment selection and clinical follow-up in ulcerative colitis (UC). Colonoscopic examination is the gold...

item.title: Serum biomarkers for predicting Crohn's disease activity: The role of bilirubin, uric acid, and the C-reactive protein/albumin ratio.
item.description: PloS one - Crohn's disease is a chronic, progressive inflammatory condition of the gastrointestinal tract that requires long-term assessment of disease activity. There is a growing need for convenient serum b...

item.title: Inflammation rewires the enteric nervous system through neurogenic monocyte recruitment.
item.description: The Journal of experimental medicine - Proper organization of the enteric nervous system (ENS) is critical for normal gastrointestinal (GI) physiology. Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) disrupts key GI functions, including bowel motility...

item.title: Sigmoid wall thickness correlates with fecal calprotectin during irritable bowel syndrome exacerbation.

item.description: Immunity, inflammation and disease - Ulcerative colitis (UC) and Helicobacter pylori (H. pylori) infection show an intriguing inverse epidemiological association, but the underlying molecular mechanisms remain unclear, with emerging e...

item.title: Distinct hepcidin patterns in Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis: links to iron homeostasis and inflammatory activity.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Multicenter study - Hepcidin, the master regulator of systemic iron metabolism, is influenced by iron availability and inflammation. In inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), the interplay between iron deficiency and infla...

item.title: Admixture and Genetic Connectivity: Autosomal Insights Into Indo-Aryan Speakers at the Eastern Edge of the Indian Subcontinent.

item.title: A Network-Based Association of IBD and Colorectal Cancer Using Proteomics Data.
item.description: Proteomics. Clinical applications - Colorectal cancer (CRC) is a major cause of morbidity and mortality, with chronic inflammation from inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) representing a well-established risk factor. Clarifying shared m...

item.title: Microbial signatures in psoriatic arthritis distinguish disease phenotypes and newly diagnosed inflammatory bowel disease independent of faecal calprotectin.

item.title: Imputation methods for serologic biomarkers in inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Scientific reports - Serologic biomarkers have emerged as a powerful tool for the diagnosis of Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) and the differentiation between subgroups of IBD. However, missingness in serologic data c...

item.title: Antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibody-associated vasculitis with initial gastrointestinal symptoms: case series and literature review.

item.title: Fecal calprotectin-guided treatment escalation strategy enhances disease clearance in ulcerative colitis.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Disease clearance may represent the ultimate treatment target in ulcerative colitis, but is seldom achieved in clinical practice. A treatment escalation strategy based on fecal calprotectin may pot...

item.title: Trichuris suis ova in inflammatory bowel disease-clinical challenges and translational pathways.
item.description: Frontiers in immunology - Review - This article reviews the mechanisms and clinical trial findings of Trichuris suis ova (TSO) for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).The results show TSO may exert potential effects on IBD via multiple...

item.title: Integrated metabolomics and genetic analyses reveal loss of protective docosahexaenoic acid as a key driver linking ultra-processed food to Crohn's disease risk.

item.description: ACS nano - Therapeutic intervention of cell-free DNA (cfDNA) scavenging via DNase I coupled with rapid dynamic repair of the compromised intestinal epithelial barrier (IEB) in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)...

item.title: Plasma Biomarkers of Senescence in Cholestatic Liver Disease: A Signature of Risk Stratification and Progression.
item.description: Journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Cellular senescence is a hallmark of several liver diseases, including primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) and primary biliary cholangitis (PBC). Senescent cholangiocytes exhibit a senescence-asso...

item.title: Postoperative Ileum Transcriptomics Implicate Sex-Biased Mechanisms in Crohn's Disease Recurrence.
item.description: Gastroenterology - Despite widespread biologic use, more than 70% of patients with Crohn's disease require resectional surgery, most commonly of the terminal ileum. Gene expression and genetics of the neoterminal ile...

item.title: Biomarkers for Disease Activity in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Gastroenterology clinics of North America - Review - This summary aims to guide clinicians in applying biomarker-based monitoring strategies for personalized, evidence-based care. It provides a comprehensive update on established and emerging blood, ...

item.title: Shared Biomarkers LCN2 and CXCL11 for Ulcerative Colitis and Colon Cancer: Bioinformatics Analysis and Diagnostic Model Construction.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Long-term chronic inflammation is an important risk factor for colon cancer. Ulcerative colitis is a complex chronic inflammatory disease. Related studies have shown that the risk of colon cancer i...

item.title: Altered peripheral CRY1 gene expression may contribute to both organic and functional gastrointestinal disease.
item.description: Experimental physiology - Gastrointestinal conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are characterized by alterations in physiological and immune functions. Given the circadian ...
